name: Cash America Pawn
description_short: Cash America Pawn Shively, Kentucky — FirstCash is the leading international pawn operator with 3,300+ locations across 29 U.S. states, the UK, and Lati...
description_long:
FirstCash, Inc. is headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas and operates as the largest international pawn shop chain with over 3,300 retail locations spanning 29 U.S. states, Washington D.C., the United Kingdom, and Latin America (Mexico, Guatemala, Colombia, and El Salvador). The company employs approximately 22,000 people and is publicly traded on both the S&P MidCap 400 Index and Russell 2000 Index, indicating significant scale and institutional credibility in the consumer lending space.

The company's primary service is non-recourse pawn loans secured by pledged personal property, allowing customers to obtain immediate cash without credit checks. FirstCash buys and sells a wide variety of merchandise including jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments. Beyond traditional pawn lending, they offer layaway services (with 10% down payment), gold and precious metal buying at spot prices, and retail merchandise sales. Through its subsidiary AFF, FirstCash also provides lease-to-own and retail finance payment solutions through 15,000+ merchant partner locations nationwide.

FirstCash distinguishes itself through its extensive geographic footprint—significantly larger than most regional pawn operators—and its dual business model combining pawn lending with point-of-sale payment solutions and lease-to-own financing. The company's inclusion in major market indices reflects institutional investor confidence. Their online store locator and inventory tools provide transparency about available merchandise and locations, and their focus on gold buying and layaway services offers alternatives to traditional pawn loans for different customer needs.

As with all pawn lenders, customers must understand that pawn loans involve surrendering personal property and face losing collateral if loans aren't repaid. Interest rates on pawn loans, while typically lower than payday loans, are not disclosed on their website and vary by state regulations. The company's size and public market status mean regulatory compliance, but consumers should still research local loan terms and redemption periods carefully before pledging valuable items.
pros: Over 3,300 locations across 29 U.S. states, D.C., UK, and Latin America provides exceptional accessibility
No credit check required for pawn loans—immediate approval based on collateral value alone
Instant cash disbursement on same day after item evaluation
Non-recourse loans mean you're never pursued for deficiency if item sells for less than loan amount
Wide variety of accepted merchandise (jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, instruments, sporting goods)
Layaway service allows purchasing items with only 10% down payment, building savings discipline
Buys gold, silver, and platinum at spot prices for immediate cash conversion
Publicly traded company (S&P MidCap 400, Russell 2000) indicates regulatory oversight and financial stability
cons: Interest rates and loan terms not disclosed on website—rates vary by state and are typically higher than traditional bank loans
Risk of losing collateral permanently if loan isn't repaid before redemption period expires
Pawn loans are short-term solutions (typically 30-90 days) and not suitable for long-term financing needs
Store inventory varies significantly by location, limiting what can be purchased or pawned at any given store
No online loan application or redemption process mentioned—requires in-person store visits
best_for: Unbanked or credit-constrained consumers who need fast cash without credit checks
People with valuable personal property (jewelry, electronics, instruments) they're willing to temporarily pledge
Consumers seeking short-term bridge loans between paychecks or unexpected expenses
Bargain hunters looking to purchase discounted used merchandise (jewelry, electronics, tools)
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for credit-constrained consumers with valuable personal property who need immediate cash and can afford to lose their collateral if unable to repay within the redemption period. The critical caveat is that pawn loans carry undisclosed interest rates (varying by state), are inherently short-term solutions, and result in permanent loss of pledged items if not redeemed—making them a stopgap measure rather than a sustainable financing strategy.
services: Non-recourse pawn loans secured by personal property
Gold, silver, and platinum buying at market prices
Retail sales of jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments
Layaway plans with 10% down payment for merchandise purchases
Store locator tool to find nearby locations
Online inventory browsing and merchandise search
Item valuation and appraisal services in-store
Lease-to-own payment solutions through subsidiary AFF network (15,000+ merchant partners)
Pawn loan FAQs and educational resources
Same-day cash disbursement upon loan approval
